C005.claim: Photons are massless but carry energy and momentum, correcting the misconception that they 'have mass'.
C005.result_snippet: The photon concept has led to momentous advances in experimental and theoretical physics, including lasers, Bose–Einstein condensation, quantum field theory, and the probabilistic interpretation of quantum mechanics. We can say photons are Quantum of electromagnetic energy. Photons always travels with speed of light. Rest mass is zero means it cannot exist at rest. We should not talk about mass of the photons. Only its energy and momentum are well defined. Photons are the quantum particles of light, central to both classical electromagnetism and quantum mechanics. A common question arises from their seemingly paradoxical nature: If photons have zero rest mass, how can they possess energy? The mass squared of emitted photon is always negative ( m2 < 0 ) = unreal photon ! So the quantum mechanics's photon model violates basic phisycal law. We have to abandon this quantum mechanics based on unreal virtual photon. For quantum mechanics to be mechanical would require that we have a mechanical explanation of quantization, but we do not.At any rate, gravity is present throughout the experiment, and though it can be ignored as a matter of relative mass, it cannot be ignored mechanically.

It assumes a basic knowledge of quantum field theory and describes the structure of the Standard Model, as well as a number of important, underlying aspects of field theory. The Standard Model is built from relativistic quantum field theories we already know about. QED is incorporated into electroweak theory, and the whole is woven together with QCD to create a single theory whose essential elements can be written in a single equation. Quantum mechanics helps people understand chemistry, because it explains how atoms interact with each other and form molecules. Many remarkable phenomena can be explained using quantum mechanics, like superfluidity. The quantum theory used to describe the micro world, and the general theory of relativity used to describe the macro world, are difficult to fit into a single framework. No one has managed to make the two mathematically compatible in the context of the Standard Model.
